title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string among an array of strings. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string "". A common prefix is defined as the longest substring that appears at the start of all given strings.

examples.explanation: The common prefix for the strings is "fl" as it appears at the start of all three strings.
There is no common prefix among the strings; hence the output is an empty string "".
The longest common prefix is "inter", which is shared among all three strings.

constraints: All input strings are lowercase letters.
The array length will be in the range [0, 200].
Each string will have a length of at most 200.
hints: Consider iterating through each character of the strings.
Keep track of the shortest string's length to limit your checks.
If a character does not match, stop checking further.
Try using a horizontal scanning approach to compare prefixes.
